rationale.R1.5: The delegation is exclusively about restructuring section ordering and narrative arc. The artifact presents four paragraphs in an inverted academic order — Results first, then Related Work, then Generative Model, then Algorithm — making R1.5 the sole load-bearing dimension. Failure to correct this dependency ordering (model and algorithm must precede results; related work typically precedes both) directly invalidates the delegation.
rationale.R1.2: Peripherally active: the Results paragraph contains a forward-reference ('the identifiability results reported in the opening paragraph'; 'Algorithm 1'; 'Proposition 2') that creates logical dependency violations under the current ordering. Any valid restructuring must respect these premise-before-consequence dependencies, so logical consistency is a constraint on, though not the goal of, the task.
rationale.R1.6: Peripherally active: reordering paragraphs will likely break transition phrases between sections (e.g., 'Concretely' and 'It is this kernel... that produces the identifiability results reported in the opening paragraph' become misaligned after restructuring). Minor transition edits may be necessary, but prose quality improvement is not the delegated goal.
